about.title: About
about.subtitle: Our Songs for Peace
about.desc1: We first sang in front of Gureombi Rock in Gangjeong Village, Jeju, where the rough winds blow. We realized that peace is not a grand slogan, but begins the moment we listen to each other's voices.
about.desc2: Gangjeong Peace Music Camp is a solidarity and festival of musicians dreaming of a world without war. Beyond borders and languages, we speak of peace with music, the most beautiful weapon.
about.desc3: When we sing, dance, and embrace each other together, war will stop and the waves of peace will spread further.

card1.title: Sustainable Peace
card1.desc: The first harmony began in the summer of 2023. Our festival will continue not as a one-time event, but as a persistent and beautiful ritual of peace guarding the summer of Gangjeong every year.

card2.title: Solidarity Without Borders
card2.desc: We remember conflict zones from the Gaza Strip in Palestine to Ukraine. Gangjeong Peace Music Camp goes beyond a simple concert; it is a powerful platform of solidarity standing with those suffering and yearning for peace.

gallery.page_desc: Moments of us singing for peace. Explore vivid photos from the Gangjeong Peace Music Camp.
gallery.section_subtitle: A record of the wind and songs of Gangjeong

camp.not_found_desc: The requested camp information could not be found.
camp.description_2026: The 3rd Gangjeong Peace Music Camp will be held from June 5 (Fri) to June 7 (Sun), 2026, at Gangjeong Sports Park in Seogwipo, Jeju. Marking the 73rd anniversary of the Korean War armistice, the camp expands for the first time into a 3-day, 2-night format with a record 54 teams of musicians. As militarization and the threat of war intensify on the Korean Peninsula, the song of peace that began in Gangjeong Village grows into a stronger and wider solidarity. Under the slogan "Let's sing, let's dance, end the war!", musicians from diverse genres deliver messages of peace and anti-war through music.
camp.slogan_2026: Let's sing, let's dance, end the war!
camp.seo_musician_suffix: Performing at the 3rd Gangjeong Peace & Music Camp, June 5-7, 2026 at Gangjeong Sports Park, Jeju.
camp.cta_final_body: Help the song of peace that began in Gangjeong reach further. Support us on Tumblbug.

gangjeong_story.section_aria_label: Why Gangjeong — The Gangjeong Village Story
gangjeong_story.hook_intro: A nearly 20-year story of peace from Gangjeong Village, Jeju. And the music festival built upon it.
gangjeong_story.timeline_2007_desc: Gangjeong Village, Seogwipo, Jeju designated as naval base site
gangjeong_story.timeline_2009_desc: Despite 94% opposition, naval base repackaged as 'tourism port'
gangjeong_story.timeline_2012_desc: Natural lava rock in UNESCO Biosphere Reserve destroyed by explosives
gangjeong_story.timeline_2015_desc: Gangjeong Village awarded IPB Sean MacBride Peace Prize — globally recognized
gangjeong_story.timeline_2016_desc: Base completed after state violence: 700+ detained, 60+ arrested, ₩400M in fines, ₩3.45B in damages claimed
gangjeong_story.timeline_2018_desc: US nuclear aircraft carrier enters Jeju Naval Base
gangjeong_story.timeline_2023_desc: Peace music camp launched at Gangjeong Sports Park
gangjeong_story.timeline_2024_desc: 'To You in a Distant Place Whose Name I Don't Know' — 13 tracks
gangjeong_story.timeline_2025_desc: Naval base upgraded to Fleet Command. US Navy vessels begin regular port calls, integrating Jeju into US military strategy
gangjeong_story.timeline_2025b_desc: Songs of peace resound again in Gangjeong
gangjeong_story.timeline_2026_desc: 10 years since base completion, largest ever 3-day festival
gangjeong_story.story_block1_text: Gureombi Rock, the symbol of Gangjeong. This lava formation, shaped by waves over tens of thousands of years, was blasted in 2012 to make way for the naval base.
gangjeong_story.story_block2_text: But the memory of people who prayed, sang, and danced on Gureombi never faded. The story of this village — where peace prayers have been offered every morning for nearly 20 years — is the root of this camp.
gangjeong_story.story_block3_text: Not with military bases, but with music. Not through confrontation, but through solidarity. Gangjeong Peace Music Camp is a festival built on this memory. Because the most beautiful resistance is singing together.
gangjeong_story.story_block3_alt: Gangjeong Peace Music Camp night festival
gangjeong_story.solidarity_declaration: From Palestine to the Korean Peninsula — as long as war does not end, our song will not end either
gangjeong_story.closing_slogan: Let's sing, let's dance, end the war!
gangjeong_story.paragraph1: In 2007, Gangjeong Village in Seogwipo, Jeju was chosen as the site for a naval base. In a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve and habitat of natural monuments, villagers and civil society sustained nearly two decades of resistance to protect their peaceful home.
gangjeong_story.paragraph2: Gureombi Rock, the symbol of Gangjeong, was destroyed during the base's construction — but the longing for peace that bloomed there never faded. The memory of people who prayed, sang, and danced on Gureombi is the root of this camp.
gangjeong_story.paragraph3: The Gangjeong Peace Music Camp was built on that memory. Not with military bases but with music, not through confrontation but through solidarity — we hope the sound of peace that began in this small village will reach the world.

album.page_title: To You in a Distant Place Whose Name I Don't Know - About Album
album.page_desc: 2024 Album Project of Gangjeong Peace Music Camp. A musical journey of 12 songs ending war and singing for peace.
album.hero_subtitle: A letter of peace sent from a world full of war and violence. The waves of music launched from Gangjeong Village by 12 teams of musicians.
album.meaning_desc: 'To You in a Distant Place Whose Name I Don't Know' is a message of solidarity sent to those suffering in conflict zones around the world. Ukraine, Gaza, and the Korean Peninsula. We don't know each other's faces or names, but our hearts wishing for peace while living in the same era are connected as one.
album.voices_desc: Rock, folk, jazz, electronic. 12 teams of musicians who empathize with the peace movement in Gangjeong Village translated peace in their own musical language. This album, created by various genres, proves that diversity is the essence of peace.
album.concert_subtitle: Check out all the records of the concerts held in Gangjeong and Seoul to commemorate the album release.
album.album_title_full: To You in a Distant Place Whose Name I Don't Know
album.musicians_page_title: Participating Musicians - To You in a Distant Place Whose Name I Don't Know
album.musicians_page_desc: 12 teams of musicians who participated in the Gangjeong Peace Music Camp album project. Artists singing for peace such as Caru, Kim Dong-san, Nam-su, and Saturday even on Monday.
album.tracks_page_title: Tracks - To You in a Distant Place Whose Name I Don't Know
album.tracks_page_desc: All tracks from the Gangjeong Peace Music Camp album. 12 songs containing messages of peace and solidarity.
album.tracks_hero_subtitle: To You in a Distant Place Whose Name I Don't Know
album.image_alt_cover: Album cover for 'To You in a Distant Place Whose Name I Don't Know'

items.q: What kind of event is Gangjeong Peace Music Camp?
items.a: Gangjeong Peace Music Camp is a musical project for peace that started in Gangjeong Village, Jeju. It is an anti-war peace music festival where musicians of various genres gather to sing and demonstrate solidarity for peace in conflict regions around the world.

items.q: When will Gangjeong Peace Music Camp be held?
items.a: The 3rd Gangjeong Peace Music Camp is scheduled to be held for 3 days from June 5 (Fri) to June 7 (Sun), 2026.

items.q: Where will Gangjeong Peace Music Camp be held?
items.a: It will be held at Gangjeong Sports Park and throughout Gangjeong Village, located in Gangjeong Village, Seogwipo-si, Jeju Special Self-Governing Province.
Musicians from various genres such as rock, folk, jazz, and electronic will participate. In the 2026 camp, 54 or more teams have been confirmed to perform.
Gangjeong Peace Music Camp aims for an open festival that anyone who loves peace can enjoy for free. However, voluntary donations and goods sales will be held for the sustainability of the event.

items.q: What is the 'To You in a Distant Place Whose Name I Don't Know' project?
items.a: 'To You in a Distant Place Whose Name I Don't Know' is the name of the 2024 omnibus album project of Gangjeong Peace Music Camp. It contains 12 songs with messages of solidarity for those suffering from war in places like Ukraine and Palestine.

items.q: Where is Gangjeong Village and how do I get there?
items.a: Gangjeong Village is located on the southern coast of Seogwipo-si, Jeju Island. You can travel from Jeju Airport by bus (about 90 minutes), taxi (about 60 minutes), or rental car. It is about 15 minutes from downtown Seogwipo.

There are no accommodation facilities within the camp venue, but there are various options including guesthouses, pensions, and hotels near Gangjeong Village and Seogwipo. We recommend booking accommodation in advance.
Yes, Gangjeong Peace Music Camp is a festival for the whole family. It is held outdoors, and children can enter for free.
If you wish to volunteer, please send a DM on Instagram (@peace_music_in_gangjeong) or contact us via email at gpmc0625@gmail.com.

items.q: What is the weather like in Jeju in June?
items.a: June in Jeju is warm with average temperatures of 20-25°C, just before the rainy season begins. Occasional rain is possible, so we recommend bringing a rain jacket or umbrella.

items.q: Where can I buy the album?
items.a: The album 'To You in a Distant Place Whose Name I Don't Know' can be purchased at Naver SmartStore (smartstore.naver.com/peaceandmusic).

items.q: What is the history of Gangjeong Village's peace movement?
items.a: Gangjeong Village has been a symbol of the peace movement for nearly 20 years since the 2007 naval base construction decision. The UNESCO-protected Gureombi Rock was blasted in 2012, and the village received the IPB Sean MacBride Peace Award in 2015. Residents have maintained daily peace prayers for over 7,000 consecutive days.

items.q: How can I apply to perform as a musician?
items.a: Musicians wishing to perform should contact us via Instagram (@peace_music_in_gangjeong) or email (gpmc0625@gmail.com). The planning team will review and provide guidance.

items.q: Is the festival accessible for people with disabilities?
items.a: Gangjeong Peace Music Camp strives to be accessible to everyone. Due to the nature of the outdoor venue, there may be some mobility limitations. Please contact us in advance via email (gpmc0625@gmail.com) and we will provide as much support as possible.

items.q: How can I support the camp financially?
items.a: You can support us through crowdfunding at Tumblbug (tumblbug.com/gpmc3). Purchasing goods or albums from our Naver SmartStore also greatly helps sustain the camp.

camp-2023.title: 1st Gangjeong Peace Music Camp
camp-2023.short_description: The first camp singing for peace in Gangjeong Village
camp-2023.description: The first Peace Music Camp held in Gangjeong Village on June 10, 2023. Under the slogan "End the war! Play, dance, and sing!", we shared solidarity and friendship within the Gangjeong peace movement.
camp-2023.location: Gangjeong Sports Park (669 Ieodo-ro, Seogwipo-si, Jeju)
camp-2023.slogan: End the war! Play, dance, and sing!

camp-2025.title: 2nd Gangjeong Peace Music Camp
camp-2025.short_description: On the 72nd anniversary of the armistice, raise the voices for peace
camp-2025.description: The second Peace Music Camp held across Gangjeong Village, Jeju, on June 14, 2025. Marking the 72nd anniversary of the armistice, it delivered a message of peace against the intensifying militarization of the Korean Peninsula.
camp-2025.location: Gangjeong Sports Park (2661, Gangjeong-dong, Seogwipo-si, Jeju) and around Gangjeong Village
camp-2025.slogan: Let's sing, let's dance, end the war!

Marking the 73rd anniversary of the Korean War armistice, the camp expands for the first time into a 3-day, 2-night format with a record 54 teams of musicians. As militarization and the threat of war intensify on the Korean Peninsula, the song of peace that began in Gangjeong Village grows into a stronger and wider solidarity. Under the slogan "Let's sing, let's dance, end the war!", musicians from diverse genres deliver messages of peace and anti-war through music.
camp-2026.slogan: Let's sing, let's dance, end the war!

structured_data.description: A project of musicians delivering a message of peace through music in a world where war and violence are prevalent
structured_data.music_group_desc: A musical project singing for peace

structured_data.howto_name: How to Attend Gangjeong Peace Music Camp
structured_data.howto_step1_text: Visit peaceandmusic.net to check the camp dates and lineup. The 3rd camp in 2026 runs from June 5 (Fri) to June 7 (Sun).
structured_data.howto_step2_text: Take a bus or taxi from Jeju Airport to Gangjeong Village in Seogwipo. Car rental is also available.
structured_data.howto_step3_text: You can support the camp through crowdfunding at tumblbug.com/gpmc3.
structured_data.howto_step4_text: Arrive at Gangjeong Sports Park and enter for free. No tickets or reservations needed.
structured_data.howto_step5_text: Watch performances by musicians of diverse genres and share in the spirit of peace solidarity.
